commit.message:
[docs]: Update copilot-instructions.md with accurate VS testbed setup guide (#23843)

### Description of PR

Summary:
Update `.github/copilot-instructions.md` with accurate and comprehensive
instructions for setting up a VS (Virtual Switch) testbed and running
tests. The previous instructions were incomplete and contained
inaccurate pytest command examples.

### Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Testbed and Framework(new/improvement)
- [ ] New Test case
    - [ ] Skipped for non-supported platforms
- [ ] Test case improvement

### Back port request
- [ ] 202205
- [ ] 202305
- [ ] 202311
- [ ] 202405
- [ ] 202411
- [ ] 202505
- [ ] 202511

### Approach
#### What is the motivation for this PR?

The existing `copilot-instructions.md` had a minimal "Build
Instructions" section that pointed users to set up a Python venv and
`pip install`, which is not how VS testing actually works. The pytest
command examples also used incorrect/incomplete flags. This file serves
as guidance for AI coding agents (GitHub Copilot) and can also be
referenced by community contributors, so accuracy is important.

#### How did you do it?

- Replaced the "Build Instructions" section with a comprehensive "Setup"
section containing:
  - Repository clone instructions
- Full 9-step VS testbed setup guide: sonic-mgmt container creation
(`setup-container.sh`), management network setup
(`setup-management-network.sh`), Ansible inventory configuration, image
preparation (cEOS from Arista, sonic-vs from public SONiC build
pipeline), environment variables, topology deployment, minigraph push,
and teardown
- Fixed the "Running Tests" section with correct pytest command flags
(`--inventory`, `--host-pattern`, `--testbed_file`, `--testbed`, `-rav`,
etc.)
- Added the `--skip_sanity --disable_loganalyzer` speedup tip

#### How did you verify/test it?

Documentation-only change. The instructions have been verified against a
working VS testbed setup workflow.

#### Any platform specific information?

N/A - documentation only

#### Supported testbed topology if it's a new test case?

N/A - documentation only

### Documentation

Updated `.github/copilot-instructions.md` which provides guidance for AI
coding agents and community contributors.

commit.message:
Feat/task 07 ml model registry (#19)

* feat: add ML model registry with ONNX and scikit-learn support (task 07)

- Add ModelRegistry for registering and retrieving ML models by name
- Add ONNXModel with lazy loading via onnxruntime and empty-schema passthrough
- Add SklearnModel with joblib/pickle loading, feature_order reordering, security warning
- Add ModelProvider protocol and ModelValidationResult frozen dataclass
- Add four new exceptions: ModelNotFoundError, ModelLoadError, ModelInputError, ModelInferenceError
- Wire ctx.predict() into BacktestEngine via model_registry param on _BacktestContext
- Add validation step 7 (Model Compatibility) via ModelCheck with AST scanning
- Extend nexa run and nexa validate CLI with --model name:path flag
- Add ONNX fixture generator script and pre-built simple_predictor.onnx fixture
- Add ml_da_algo.py example showing end-to-end ML workflow
- Add 50 comprehensive tests across 16 test classes (633 total, 99% coverage)
- Export new types from top-level nexa_backtest package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard sharpe ratio against single-observation std dev (ddof=1 with n=1)

Require at least 2 return observations before calling np.std(ddof=1).
With only 1 return value, numpy emits a RuntimeWarning (degrees of
freedom <= 0) and produces NaN. Returns None early instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: clean up model loading and validation logic

- SklearnModel._load(): wrap the entire joblib/pickle load block in a
  single try/except so errors from both loaders are caught and wrapped
  in ModelLoadError (previously pickle.load() errors could escape unwrapped)
- SklearnModel.validate(): remove redundant (ModelLoadError, Exception)
  — Exception already covers ModelLoadError
- model_check.py: replace fragile string-based failure detection
  ('FAILED' in m) with an explicit boolean flag
- _find_predict_calls(): use dict for O(1) deduplication instead of
  O(n) list membership checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update poetry.lock

* Improve code coverage

* Trigger build

* fix ruff checks

commit.message:
🗃️ feat: semantic intent filter for orientation-specific queries 🗃️

Add QueryIntent detection and ResultMatchesIntent filter so that
orientation-specific queries (e.g. "lesbian", "girl on girl", "sapphic")
reject results whose titles clearly indicate biological male participation.
Changes:
- taxonomy.go: add QueryIntent struct, DetectQueryIntent(), and
ResultMatchesIntent() with conservative whole-word matching
- femaleOnlyTerms: triggers female-only mode for 12 orientation terms
- malePresenceWords: 14 explicit-male tokens (cock/dick/bbc/stepbro/
facial/cumshot/handjob/etc); strapon/dildo intentionally excluded
- containsWholeWord(): safe word-boundary checker preventing
false positives like "german" matching "man"
- DetectQueryIntent(): also populates HasAgeTypes (teen/milf/mature)
for future cross-type validation
- manager.go: detect query intent once before result collection loop;
apply ResultMatchesIntent after existing term-match filter
Conservative design principles:
- Only filter when contradiction is unambiguous in the title
- "strapon", "dildo", "strap-on" are NOT male indicators
- Word-boundary checks prevent substring false positives
- Has no effect on non-orientation queries (zero overhead)

commit.message:
chore(*): use shared tsconfig base and TypeScript v6 (#981)

This updates the repo to consume the shared `tsconfig.base.json` pattern
used in `lumirlumir-configs` instead of duplicating compiler settings
across packages. It also moves TypeScript references to v6 and keeps
`eslint-config-bananass` on the latest published version required for
that upgrade.

- **Shared TypeScript base**
- Adds a root `tsconfig.base.json` aligned with the shared config
repository.
- Moves common compiler options out of package-level `tsconfig.json`
files and into the shared base.
- Keeps package-local overrides only where they are actually
package-specific (`rootDir`, `outDir`, test-only flags).

- **Reference sync updates**
- Updates `.github/sync-client.yml` to sync `tsconfig.base.json` to
`lumirlumir-configs`.
- Removes the old pattern of treating package `tsconfig.json` files as
the synced source of truth.

- **TypeScript v6 rollout**
  - Upgrades root and template TypeScript references from v5 to v6.
  - Refreshes the lockfile accordingly.
- Preserves `eslint-config-bananass` at the latest version during the
dependency update.

- **TypeScript example/template compatibility**
- Updates TypeScript example and template configs for the v6 behavior
change.
- Adds the minimal overrides needed for the example projects to continue
typechecking cleanly under v6.
- Adjusts the grade lookup example to avoid the stricter indexed-access
error surfaced by the new compiler/runtime combo.

Example of the config consolidation:

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "outDir": "build",
    "skipLibCheck": true
  }
}
```

---

References:

- https://github.com/lumirlumir/npm-eslint-markdown/pull/533
- https://github.com/lumirlumir/npm-clang-format-node/pull/603

---------

commit.message:
fix: add Ctrl+J as universal newline key for multiline input

Most macOS terminals (Terminal.app, iTerm2) send identical \r for both
Enter and Shift+Enter — there's no way to distinguish them. Only Kitty
and WezTerm support the Kitty keyboard protocol (\x1b[13;2u).

Fix: Add Ctrl+J (\n / LF) as cross-terminal newline. In raw mode, Enter
sends \r (CR) while Ctrl+J sends \n (LF), making them distinguishable
on ALL terminal emulators.

Newline options:
- Ctrl+J: Works everywhere (recommended)
- Shift+Enter: Works on Kitty/WezTerm (Kitty protocol)
- Alt+Enter: Works on iTerm2 and some terminals
- Backslash + Enter: Always works (manual continuation)

Also: added multiline hint to banner UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: avoid HashSet allocation in role requirement handlers (#221)

Replace Intersect().Any() with Any(Contains) in RealmAccessRequirementHandler
and ResourceAccessRequirementHandler. The Enumerable.Intersect() method
internally allocates a HashSet on every call, which is unnecessary overhead
in the hot authorization path (called once per authenticated request per
protected endpoint).

For typical role lists (1-10 items), direct Contains checks are faster
due to lower allocation pressure and better cache locality.

perf: single-pass org claims scan and avoid ToArray in VerificationPlan (#238)

- KeycloakOrganizationClaimsExtensions.GetOrganizations: replace the
  three-step Where→ToList / FirstOrDefault / Where→Select→ToList pattern
  with a single foreach over the claims collection. The JSON claim is
  detected eagerly and short-circuits the loop; string aliases are
  lazily accumulated in a List<string>? to avoid allocation when there
  are no matching claims at all.

- VerificationPlan: change the internal scopes store from
  Dictionary<string,List<string>> to Dictionary<string,string[]>. This
  lets GetEnumerator() pass the stored array directly to
  ProtectedResourceAttribute instead of calling .ToArray() on every
  iteration, eliminating one heap allocation per resource per
  enumeration. The Add() path handles the rare multi-scope case with a
  simple array copy.

commit.message:
feat(authorization): add Audience override for per-resource UMA ticket exchange (#135) (#246)

Allows a single .NET API to enforce policies from multiple Keycloak
resource server clients (same realm) by specifying an optional Audience
on ProtectedResourceAttribute and DecisionRequirement.

When Audience is set, it is forwarded through ProtectedResourceVerifier
and IAuthorizationServerClient down to AuthorizationServerClient.PrepareRequest,
which uses it as the UMA 'audience' parameter instead of the global
KeycloakAuthorizationServerOptions.Resource value.

When Audience is null, behaviour is fully backward-compatible.

* fix: guard sharpe ratio against single-observation std dev (ddof=1 with n=1)

Require at least 2 return observations before calling np.std(ddof=1).
With only 1 return value, numpy emits a RuntimeWarning (degrees of
freedom <= 0) and produces NaN. Returns None early instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: clean up model loading and validation logic

- SklearnModel._load(): wrap the entire joblib/pickle load block in a
  single try/except so errors from both loaders are caught and wrapped
  in ModelLoadError (previously pickle.load() errors could escape unwrapped)
- SklearnModel.validate(): remove redundant (ModelLoadError, Exception)
  — Exception already covers ModelLoadError
- model_check.py: replace fragile string-based failure detection
  ('FAILED' in m) with an explicit boolean flag
- _find_predict_calls(): use dict for O(1) deduplication instead of
  O(n) list membership checks

commit.message:
eng: add startup validators for Admin and Protection client options (#239)

Add IValidateOptions<T> implementations for KeycloakAdminClientOptions and
KeycloakProtectionClientOptions, following the existing pattern established
by KeycloakTokenIntrospectionOptionsValidator.

- KeycloakAdminClientOptionsValidator: validates AuthServerUrl is a valid
  absolute URI and Realm is configured
- KeycloakProtectionClientOptionsValidator: same checks for the protection
  client
- Register validators with .ValidateOnStart() so misconfiguration is caught
  at app startup rather than on first HTTP request
- Add unit tests covering success and failure paths

Previously, misconfigured options would throw a NullReferenceException inside
the HttpClient factory when the first request was made (the code used
AuthServerUrl! null-forgiving). Now developers get a clear, actionable error
message at startup.
